data.content:
The build confirms the file is generated but not compiled. The `GenerateSampleCode` target writes the `.cs` file but never adds it to the `@(Compile)` item group, so the compiler doesn't see it.

**Fix:** Add the generated file to `Compile` items within the target:

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target runs `BeforeTargets="CoreCompile;BeforeCompile"` and generates the file, but the generated file is never added to the `Compile` item group. The MSBuild compilation picks up source files from `@(Compile)` items, but since the file is generated during the build into the intermediate output path, it's not automatically included.

The fix is to add the generated file to the `Compile` item group within the target, after it's been created. I need to add:

```xml
<ItemGroup>
  <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />


</ItemGroup>
```

to the `GenerateSampleCode` target.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

  </Target>

data.content:
**Root cause:** The `GenerateSampleCode` target generated the `.cs` file to the intermediate output directory, but never added it to the `@(Compile)` item group. MSBuild only compiles files in that item group, so the generated file was silently ignored.

**Fix:** Added an `<ItemGroup><Compile Include="..." /></ItemGroup>` inside the target so the generated file is included in compilation.
